counter_increment_patterns.summary: Counters are initialized in bin/stampede.sh, but runtime increments are delegated to the commander agent with no repository-enforced atomic read-modify-write logic.
evidence: bin/stampede.sh:771-831 initializes telemetry counters at 0 and writes swarm-state.json directly
agents/stampede-commander.agent.md:57 requires the commander to update swarm-state.json after child launches
risks: Lost updates if multiple child transitions race on stale state
Drift between telemetry.* and launch_proof.* fallback fields
Blocked/failed overwrite paths can preserve inconsistent counters

running_completed_transitions.summary: There is no code-enforced transition system for running/completed; real runs show stale and contradictory values.
evidence: run-20260430-180646 commander-005 swarm-state.json shows squad_leads_running=11 and squad_leads_completed=39 while child-agents.jsonl has 50 launch_started and 50 completed records
bin/stampede-monitor.sh:162-165 computes display strictly from swarm-state.json telemetry
bin/stampede.sh:982-997 marks commander_launch_failed without reconciling counters
mismatches: Failure status can coexist with inflated completion counts in other runs
Completed display may mask stale atoms_received via fallback logic

failed_counter_handling.summary: Failure counters exist, but failure persistence and interaction with running/completed are not programmatically guaranteed.
evidence: bin/stampede-monitor.sh:164 falls back from workers_failed to children_failed
agents/stampede-commander.agent.md documents completed events but does not define a strict failure reconciliation algorithm
risks: Timeout/partial/failure events may not decrement running consistently
Failures depend on commander self-reporting rather than reconciliation from ledger/files

atoms_received_accuracy.summary: atoms_received is initialized and displayed, but no repository code computes it from atom files or ledger events; it can remain 0 despite existing atoms.
evidence: agents/stampede-commander.agent.md:124 expects atoms_received to reach 250 on success
run-20260430-180646 commander-005 swarm-state.json:53 shows atoms_received=0 despite multiple atom synthesis files in the atoms directory

telemetry_consistency.summary: swarm-state.json, child-agents.jsonl, and on-disk atom artifacts are not reconciled from a single source of truth.
evidence: bin/stampede-monitor.sh reads swarm-state.json only for nested telemetry
child-agents.jsonl contains append-only launch/completed records that can outpace state updates
No repository code cross-checks telemetry counters against child-agents.jsonl or atoms/ contents
telemetry_consistency.highest_priority_issue: atoms_received and completion totals can diverge from actual atom files on disk while monitor output still looks healthy.

overall_verdict.result: swarm-state.json counters are directionally useful but not reliable as accurate live truth without reconciliation.
overall_verdict.recommended_source_of_truth: Use child-agents.jsonl plus filesystem artifact counts to validate swarm-state.json telemetry.

summary: Telemetry accuracy is weak. swarm-state.json is initialized correctly but runtime counters rely on commander self-reporting, not enforced atomic updates or reconciliation. running/completed/failed semantics can drift from child-agents.jsonl, and atoms_received is especially unreliable because the repo does not derive it from actual atom files. Treat swarm-state.json as advisory, not authoritative.
